feed.title: The Nature Conservancy Seeks Stewardship Technician for Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Nature Conservancy has posted an opening for a Stewardship Technician at the Randall Preserve. The role encompasses diverse responsibilities, including maintaining preserve areas frequented by the public, removing exotic species, monitoring wildlife, managing tools and equipment, overseeing building maintenance, and assisting with working cattle ranch infrastructure. The technician will also be involved in operating small equipment and maintaining the preserve's sensor network.

feed.title: Randall Preserve Secures Proposition 4 Funds for Climate and Recreation
feed.summary: The California Grants Portal announced that funds from Proposition 4 are now available for the Randall Preserve. These funds are specifically allocated to support initiatives that reduce climate impacts on disadvantaged communities and vulnerable populations, and to create, protect, and expand outdoor recreation opportunities within the preserve. Eligible entities for this funding include nonprofit organizations, public agencies, and tribal governments, with funds available for encumbrance or expenditure until June 30, 2028.

feed.title: NMUSD Advances Housing Project Near Randall Preserve with 196 Units
feed.summary: The Coastal Corridor Alliance website indicates that the Newport-Mesa Unified School District (NMUSD) has advanced its housing development plan for land adjacent to the Randall Preserve. This update, dated March 16, 2026, now specifies 196 housing units, a change from previously reported figures of 169 units. This development continues to spark community discussion regarding the balance between housing needs and environmental preservation.

feed.title: Randall Preserve Secures Proposition 4 Funds for Climate and Recreation
feed.summary: The California Grants Portal has announced that funds appropriated under Proposition 4 are now available for the Randall Preserve. These funds are specifically allocated for initiatives that aim to reduce climate impacts on disadvantaged communities and vulnerable populations, and to create, protect, and expand outdoor recreation opportunities within the preserve. The funding is accessible to eligible nonprofit organizations, public agencies, and tribal governments, with availability for encumbrance or expenditure until June 30, 2028.

feed.title: Public Invited to Help Shape Future of Randall Preserve as New Era Begins
feed.summary: A recent article in Voice of OC by Meincke signals a new era for the Randall Preserve, emphasizing public involvement in shaping its future. The piece extends an invitation for the community to contribute to the ongoing planning process for the preserve. While also touching upon environmental groups' concerns regarding an Orange County school project, the primary focus highlights the broader public engagement for the preserve's development.

feed.title: Blogger Solicits Community Opinion on NMUSD Housing Project Near Randall Preserve
feed.summary: Local real estate blogger Jeb Smith discussed the Newport-Mesa Unified School District's recent decision to move forward with a housing development on 11 acres adjacent to the Randall Preserve. The post highlights the ongoing debate between prioritizing workforce housing and preserving environmentally sensitive land, asking readers to share their opinions on the matter. This indicates an active community discussion about the land's future.

feed.title: Newport-Mesa Unified School District Advances Housing Project Near Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Newport-Mesa Unified School District board voted 4-2 to recognize Knighthall Capital LLC as the preferred bidder for a 99-year lease of an 11-acre plot adjacent to the Randall Preserve, formerly known as Banning Ranch. This decision advances a proposal to construct 169 housing units, with 20% designated for low-income NMUSD staff. Environmental groups, including the Mountains and Recreation Conservation Authority, expressed concerns about the loss of natural open space, advocating for the land to be added to the preserve.

feed.title: Newport-Mesa Unified School District Selects Preferred Bidder for Housing Project Near Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Newport-Mesa Unified School District board voted 4-2 to recognize Knighthall Capital LLC as the preferred bidder for a 99-year lease of an 11-acre plot adjacent to the Randall Preserve. This decision advances a proposal to construct 169 housing units, with 20% designated for low-income NMUSD staff. Environmental groups, including the Mountains and Recreation Conservation Authority, expressed concerns about the loss of natural open space, advocating for the land to be added to the preserve.

feed.title: Newport-Mesa Unified School District Advances Plan for Housing Development Near Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Newport-Mesa Unified School District board voted 4-2 to recognize Knighthall Capital LLC as the preferred bidder for a 99-year lease of an 11-acre plot at 980 16th Street, adjacent to the Randall Preserve. This decision moves forward a proposal to build 169 housing units, with up to 20% designated for low-income NMUSD staff. Environmental groups, including the Mountains and Recreation Conservation Authority, have voiced concerns and proposed an alternative plan to add the land to the preserve, while the district cites a critical need for revenue, with the lease expected to generate substantial funds.

feed.title: Newport-Mesa Unified School District Moves Forward with Housing Development Plan Adjacent to Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Newport-Mesa Unified School District (NMUSD) board voted 4-2 to recognize Knighthall Capital LLC as the preferred bidder for a 99-year lease of an 11-acre plot adjacent to the Randall Preserve. This decision moves forward a proposal to build 169 units of housing, with 20% dedicated to low-income NMUSD staff. Environmental groups, including the Mountains and Recreation Conservation Authority, advocated for the land to be added to the preserve, but their alternative plan was not chosen.

feed.title: California Coastal Commission Ecologist's Role in Conserving Randall Preserve Highlighted
feed.summary: A California Coastal Commission staff ecologist, Jonna, has been recognized for her instrumental contributions over the past decade in applying sound scientific principles to policy and decisions, particularly in conserving the Randall Preserve at Newport Banning Ranch. The preserve is notably one of the last intact wetland-bluff ecosystems remaining along the southern California coast.

feed.title: Coastal Corridor Alliance Expands Mission and Launches New Store to Support Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Coastal Corridor Alliance, formerly Banning Ranch Conservancy, has updated its mission to broadly focus on shielding and rejuvenating ecosystems within the Santa Ana River Coastal Corridor, including the Randall Preserve. The organization also announced the launch of a new merchandise store, with proceeds supporting their conservation and restoration efforts. Additionally, they continue to host monthly volunteer activities such as Coastal Discovery Tours, Fairview Park Restoration Days, and Beach & River Mouth Cleanups.

feed.title: Newport-Mesa School District Considers Housing Development on Land Adjacent to Randall Preserve
feed.summary: The Newport-Mesa Unified School District (NMUSD) is evaluating proposals to develop an 11.36-acre parcel of land near the Randall Preserve into housing, with proposals reaching up to 402 units. Environmental groups, including the Coastal Corridor Alliance, submitted a bid to acquire and preserve the land, but it was rejected. Concerns have also been raised by environmental groups about potential violations of the Surplus Land Act by the district during this process.

feed.title: Community Input Session Announced for Randall Preserve Restoration Plans
feed.summary: The Randall Preserve steering committee invites local residents to participate in an upcoming public input session. This meeting will focus on gathering community feedback and ideas for the ecological restoration and public access plans currently under development. It's a key opportunity to shape the future of the preserve.

feed.title: Randall Preserve Awarded Major Grant for Habitat Restoration
feed.summary: The Randall Preserve has secured a significant grant from the California Wildlife Conservation Board. This funding will directly support critical habitat restoration projects, including native plant revegetation and water quality improvements, accelerating the preserve's ecological recovery. This investment highlights ongoing efforts to enhance biodiversity within the region.

feed.title: Community Feedback Informs Initial Randall Preserve Master Plan Concepts
feed.summary: Following the successful May 10th community input session, the Randall Preserve Steering Committee reports a significant volume of valuable feedback from local residents. Key themes around trail design, native habitat restoration, and educational opportunities are now being integrated into the preliminary master plan concepts. This input is crucial for shaping the preserve's future.
